mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production signals robust manufacturing activity and economic expansion, which boosts corporate earnings and equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are not positive in at least 55% of months when INDPRO is above its 12-month moving average over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the unemployment rate indicates deteriorating labor market conditions, which reduces consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to negative equity returns over the subsequent month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month period
failure_condition: If 20-day forward returns are above zero in more than 50% of instances when UNRATE trigger is active over a 5-year backtest, the hypothesis is refuted.

mechanism_narrative: When the central bank raises interest rates amid elevated inflation, it tightens financial conditions and increases discount rates, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The median 60-day forward return in trigger periods is not negative or less negative than in a matched sample of non-trigger periods (p-value > 0.10 by Mann-Whitney U test).

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDP growth in early expansion phases reflects accelerating aggregate demand, driving corporate sales and earnings upward, which supports positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: If 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 40% of quarters classified as expansionary (GDP growth > 3% YoY) over any 10-year window, the hypothesis fails.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts signals a weakening housing market and broader economic slowdown, reducing construction-related employment and investment, which leads to negative equity returns over the following month.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines by more than 10% over a 3-month period
failure_condition: Twenty-day forward returns following HOUST decline triggers are not negative on average (one-sample t-test p-value > 0.05) over a 10-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Robust payroll growth combined with a low unemployment rate indicates a tight labor market that supports consumer spending and corporate profits, driving positive short-term equity returns.
candidate_trigger: both PAYEMS monthly change exceeds 200,000 and UNRATE is below 4.0%
failure_condition: If the average 5-day forward return in trigger periods is not greater than the average in non-trigger periods by at least 0.5% over a 5-year evaluation, the hypothesis is refuted.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales reflect strong consumer demand, which drives corporate revenues and earnings growth, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over a monthly horizon as investors price in improving fundamentals.
failure_condition: The rank information coefficient (IC) of RSAFS month-over-month growth with 20-day forward returns is below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation accelerating above a critical threshold signals overheating and imminent monetary tightening, which raises discount rates and depresses equity valuations, producing negative forward returns.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of trigger events observed over a 10-year period, the hypothesis is rejected.
